message:
🐛 Add auto-refresh for Copilot auth tokens at runtime (#11090)

When a Copilot CLI invocation fails with an auth error, the provider now
automatically attempts to refresh the GitHub token via `gh auth token`
and retries the command once. This handles the common case where a user
runs `gh auth refresh` in another terminal but kc-agent continues using
the stale token.

Key changes:
- Add retry-on-auth-error logic in StreamChatWithProgress (#11079)
- Strip stale GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN from subprocess env so the copilot
  CLI reads fresh tokens from gh's config/keyring
- Rate-limit refresh attempts with a 30s cooldown
- Add comprehensive tests for auth detection, env filtering, and cooldown

Signed-off-by: ks-ci-bot <ks-ci-bot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ks-ci-bot <ks-ci-bot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
